_provenance.stage: Stage 6 — Synthesis (retroactive rebuild from Codex Stage 3)
version_history: v3 (2026-05-17): retroactive editorial pass. Stage 3 (Codex) FAS=62 with 7 verified claims. Act 872 anchor added (the Bill's enacted name). Labour-force figures corrected to DOSM Q1 2025 (17.23M LF, 16.70M employed, 3.45M informal — was 16.7M LF). Gig-worker count framed as 'MOHR cited 1.2M during passage; later MOHR FAQ cites MDEC 2024 1.64M; methodologies differ' rather than asserting equivalence. '1.25% per-ride' (TOO NARROW per Stage 3) → 'per-transaction deduction from gig-worker income'. Platform-coverage list updated to official categories (p-hailing, e-hailing, freelancers, online services, other platform-mediated services); named-platform list (Grab, Foodpanda) dropped per Stage 3 (Act does not name specific companies). View card softened from 'closed the accident gap / which government dares to legislate it' to 'establishes mandatory platform-collected SOCSO; retirement coverage and timeline remain open'.

revision_log: CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Headline 'Brings ~1.2 Million Into SOCSO' (TOO ASSERTIVE on coverage number — methodologies differ) → 'SOCSO Coverage Extended, EPF Retirement Still Missing' (states coverage extension as fact without staking on a specific magnitude).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Hook 'brings ~1.2 million riders and freelancers' → 'extends SOCSO self-employment coverage to platform workers' (Stage 3: 'not that SOCSO protection reaches gig workers as soon as the Act passes; effective registration is the operational question').

CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — '1.25% per-ride deduction' → '1.25% per-transaction deduction from gig-worker income' (Stage 3: per-ride is too narrow because the Act covers p-hailing, freelancers, online services).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Fact 1 sub coverage list updated to official categories: p-hailing, e-hailing, freelancers, online services and other platform-mediated services.

REMOVED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Named-company list 'Grab, Foodpanda and other platforms must provide written terms' dropped. The Act requires platforms to inform workers of agreed terms; specific companies are not named in statute.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): INCORRECT — '16.7 million labour force in Q1 2025' → 'DOSM Q1 2025: 17.23 million labour force, 16.70 million employed, 3.45 million informal' (Stage 3 corrected the labour-force vs employed-persons distinction).

CORRECTED (Stage 3): UNVERIFIED — 'DOSM and ILO sources put the gig-worker count at ~1.2 million' replaced with 'MOHR cited 1.2 million during passage; later MOHR FAQ cites MDEC 2024 ~1.64 million on digital platforms. Methodologies differ' (sourced and methodologically honest).
REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — Reframe 'The no-protection framing is obsolete' → 'The no-social-security-protection framing is now narrower' (more honest about the partial nature of the reform).

REPHRASED (Stage 3): MISLEADING — View 'Phase 1 closed the accident gap. The Phase 2 question is...which government dares to legislate it' → 'Phase 1 establishes mandatory platform-collected SOCSO deductions. The open Phase 2 question is whether retirement-savings coverage follows, and on what timeline' (Stage 3: 'not closed the accident gap' / EPF Phase 2 should be framed as analysis, not asserted).

PRESERVED (Stage 3 VERIFIED): Bill passage dates (Dewan Rakyat August 2025, Dewan Negara 9 September 2025); first dedicated gig-worker legal framework characterization; mandatory SOCSO accident and disability protection; platforms register workers and remit contributions; EPF retirement contributions NOT included in first phase; voluntary i-Saraan ~10.7% coverage by 2022.
